title: The One With the Baby on the Bus

lines.text: Originally written by Betsy Borns
At Monica and Rachel's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who da wenny-Benny boy? You the Wenny-wenny-Benny-Benny boy, yes. Don't cry. Don't cry. Why is he still crying?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me hold him for a sec. There. (Ben stops crying) Huh? There we are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maye it's me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't be silly. Ben loves you. He's just being Mr. Crankypants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I once dated a Miss Crankypants. Lovely girl, kinda moody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There we go. All better. (gives Ben back to Monica)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There's my little boy. (Ben starts crying again)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can I uh see something? (Takes Ben. When he puts him close to Monica, Ben cries. When he moves Ben away, he stops crying.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cool.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He hates me. My nephew hates me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, don't do this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What if my own baby hates me? Huh? What am I gonna do then?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, will you stop? This is nuts. Do you know how long it's gonna be before you actually have to deal with this problem? I mean, you don't even have a boyfriend yet. Joey, she does not look fat.
Chandler has a basketball which he is moving closer to, then away from, Monica

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Goo, goo, goo, waaah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is so funny. Let me see that. (throws the ball out the window)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you ok, Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know. What's in this pie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, I don't know, butter, eggs, flour, lime, kiwi--

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Kiwi? Kiwi? I thought it was a key lime pie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No I didn't, I said kiwi lime. That's what makes it so special.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that's what's gonna kill me. I'm allergic to kiwi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you're not. You're, you're allergic to lobster and peanuts and--oh my god.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh. It's definitely getting worse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is your tongue swelling up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Either that or my mouth is getting smaller.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, get your coat, we're going to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is he gonna be ok?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, he's just gotta get a shot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, you know, actually it's getting better. It is. It is. Let's not go. Anyone for Thcrabble?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Jacket now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What about Ben? We can't bring a baby to a hospital.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We'll watch him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think tho.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? I have seven Catholic sisters. I've taken care of hundreds of kids. Come on, we wanna do it, don't we?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was looking forward to playing basketball, but I guess that's out the window.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, well, if you do take him out for his walk, you might wanna bring his hat, and there's extra milk in the fridge, and there's extra diapers in the bag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hat, milk, got it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ??? (speech garbled) Thro up a thro thro--a thro thro!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You understood that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, my uncle Sal has a really big tongue.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is he the one with the beautiful wife?
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Rach, wanna hear the new song I'm thinkin' of singing this afternoon? I wrote it this morning in the shower.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) I'm in the shower and I'm writing a song. Stop me if you've heard it. My skin is soapy, and my hair is wet, and Tegrin spelled backward is Nirget.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: Uh, Rachel, sweetheart, could I see ya for a minute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: F.Y.I.. I've decided to pay a professional musician to play in here on Sunday afternoons. Her name is Stephanie... something. She's supposed to be very good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But what about Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: Rachel, it's not that your friend is bad, it's that she's so bad, she makes me want to put my finger through my eye into my brain and swirl it around.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, ok, so you're not a fan, but I mean, come on, you cannot do this to her.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: Uh--

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no no no no. Oh no no no no. I have to do this to her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) Lather, rinse, repeat, and lather, rinse, repeat, and lather, rinse, repeat, as needed.
Chandler and Joey are loaded down with baby stuff, and Ben

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I don't think we brought enough stuff. Did you forget to pack the baby's anvil?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's gonna be worth it. It's a known fact that women love babies, all righ? Women love guys who love babies. It's that whole sensitive thing. Quick, aim him at that pack o' babes over there. Maybe one of them will break away. No, no wait, for get them, we got one, hard left. All right, gimme the baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I got him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, seriously.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, seriously you want him?

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Boys
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: And who is this little cutie pie?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, don't, don't think me immodest, but, me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You wanna smell him?

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: I assume we're talking about the baby now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah. He's got that great baby smell. Get a whiff of his head.

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: I think my uterus just skipped a beat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler) What'd I tell you? What'd I tell you?

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: I think it's great you guys are doing this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we are great guys.

lines.speaker: Caroline
lines.text: You know, my brother and his boyfriend have been trying to adopt for three years. What agency did you two go through?
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, but this is my gig. This is where I play. My, my name is written out there in chalk. You know, you can't just erase chalk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And he's going to be paying this woman? Why doesn't he just give her like a throne, and a crown, and like a, you know, gold stick with a ball on top.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Terry is a jerk, ok? That's why we're always saying "Terry's a jerk!" That's where that came from.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, ok. You probably did everything you could.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, you know what, lemme, let me just see what else I can do. All right, look, look. Why don't you just let her go on after Stephanie whatever-her-name-is. I mean, you won't even be here. You don't pay her. It's not gonna cost you anything.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: I, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, Terry, I'll even clean the cappuccino machine.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: You don't clean the cappuccino machine?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course I clean it. I mean, I,I will cleeeean it. I mean, I will cleeeean it.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: Oh, all right, fine, fine, fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Who's workin' for you babe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Oh my god. This is so exciting. How much am I gonna get?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well you said that he's paying the people who are playing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no, no no. I meant that he's gonna be paying that other woman beause she's a professional.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm not gonna be the only one who's not getting paid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, but Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, huh uh, I'm sorry, no. No, I'm not some like sloppy second, charity band. You know what, there are thousands of places in this city where people would be happy to pay to hear me play. (Out on the sidewalk, singing) When I play, I play for me, I don't need your charity. (Someone puts a coin in her guitar case) Thank you! La la la la la la la....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, there's no way I'm gonna get a shot. Maybe they can take the needle and thquirt it into my mouth, you know, like a thquirt gun.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Hello, there. I'm Dr. Carlin. I see someone's having an allergic reaction.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Doctor, can I see you for just a minute please? My brother has a slight phobia about needles.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you tell him about my thquirt gun idea?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My brother, the PhD would like to know if there's any way to treat this orally.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: No, under these circumstances it has to be an injection, and it has to be now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tho?
Monica shakes her head.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's good, have a seat. Um, the doctor says it's gotta be a needle. You're just gonna have to be brave, ok? Can you do that for me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok. Oh boy. You are doin' so good. You wanna squeeze my hand? All right, Ross, don't squeeze it so hard. Honey, really, don't squeeze it so hard! Oh, Ross! Let go of my hand!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a good plan, Joe. Next time we wanna pick up women, we should just go to the park and make out. Taxi, taxi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, look at that talent.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to taxi driver) Just practicing. You're good. Carry on.
GIRL 1 ON BUS: Hey, you. He's just adorable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, but can you tell him that, because he thinks he's too pink.
GIRL 2 ON BUS: So what are you guys out doing today?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh we're not out. No, no. We're just uh, two heterosexual guys, hanging with the son of our other heterosexual friend, doin' the usual straight guy stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You done?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
GIRL 1: Oh, there's our stop.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Get outta here. This is our stop too.
GIRL 2: You guys live around here too?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, yeah, sure. We live in the building by the uh sidewalk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, look, since we're neighbors and all, what do you say we uh, get together for a drink?
GIRL 1: So uh, you wanna go to Marquel's?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, sure, they love us over there.
GIRL 2: Where's your baby?

lines.speaker: Chandler And Joey
lines.text: (running after bus) Ben! Ben! Ben!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's good. Maybe he'll hear you and pull the cord.

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Stop the bus! Wait! Wait! Wait!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure he didn't break it because it really hurts.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: No, it's just a good bone bruise. And, right here is the puncture wound from your ring.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I'm really sorry. Sorry. Sorry! Hey! Hey! I got my s's back! Which we can celebrate later. Celebrate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) ... with the double double double-jointed boy. Hey. So um, are you the professional guitar player?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah. I'm Stephanie.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. My name was on there, but now it just says "carrot cake". So, um, so um, how many chords do you know?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: All of them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, so you know D?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, do you know A minor?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, do you know how to go from D to A minor?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok. Um, so does your guitar have a strap?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Mine does. (singing) Stephanie knows all the chords. (makes a face)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on pay phone) Come on, pick up, pick up! Hello? Transit Authority? Yes, hello. I'm doing research for a book, and I was wondering what someone might do if they left a baby on a city bus. Yes I do realize that would be a very stupid charact er.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi, here's the deal. We lost a carseat on a bus today. It's white plastic, with a handle, and it fits onto a stroller. Oh, and there was a baby in it. He wants to talk to you again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, everybody, let's give a uh nice warm Central Perk welcome to--

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing angrily) Terry's a jerk, and he won't let me work, and I hate Central Perk!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, to Stephanie Schiffer.

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Thank you. I'd like to start with a song that I wrote for the first man I ever loved. (singing) Zachary.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing/screaming) You're all invited to bite me!

lines.speaker: Chandler And Joey
lines.text: Hi. We're the guys who called about the baby. We left the baby on ths bus. Is he here? Is he here?

lines.speaker: Transit Authority Guy
lines.text: He's here. (Chandler and Joey hug each other in relief) I'm assuming one of you is the father.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually, uh, we're both the father. (Puts his arm around Joey)
BOTH (but to different babies): Oh, Ben! Hey, buddy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please tell me you know which one is our baby.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, well that one has ducks on his t-shirt, and this one has clowns. And Ben was definitely wearing ducks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or clowns. Oh, oh wait. That one's definitely Ben. Remember, he had that cute little mole by his mouth.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, Ben, remember us? Ok, the mole came off.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What're we gonna do? What're we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, uh, we'll flip for it. Ducks or clowns.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, we're gonna flip for the baby?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got a better idea?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, call it in the air.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Heads.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Heads it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Whew!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We have to assign heads to something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right. Ok, ok, uh, ducks is heads, because ducks have heads.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What kind of scary-ass clowns came to your birthday?
on the sidewalk outside Central Perk

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here. I thought you might be cold.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa, look at you, you did pretty well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eight dollars and 27 cents. But not really, 'cause I put in the first two, just to, you know, get the ball rolling, and to make myself feel better.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. This whole like playing-for-money thing is so not good for me. You know, I don't know, when I sang "Su-Su-Suicide", I got a dollar seventy-five. But then, "Smelly Cat", I got 25 cents and a condom. So you know, now I just feel really bad for Smelly Cat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you know, honey, I don't think everybody gets Smelly Cat. You know, I mean, if all you've ever actually had are healthy pets, then, whoosh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not even that. I used to do my songs because it made me happy, but now it's like, it's just all about the money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, people missed you in there. And in fact, there was actually a request for "Smelly Cat".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? From who?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, from me. And I know it's not your big money song, but it's my favorite.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: Hi. Uh, did I accidentally drop a condom in your case? It's kind of an emergency.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: Thanks a lot. Hey Christine, I got it!
chez Monica and Rachel

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just wanna thank you for being there for me today. And I'm sorry I,I almost broke your hand.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's ok. I'm sorry I poisoned you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Hey, remember the time I jammed that pencil into your hand?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Remember it? What do you think this is, a freckle?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, what about the time I hit you in the face with the Silvian's pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, man. Oh, remember when I stuck that broom in your bike spokes, and you flipped over and hit your head on the curb?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. But I remember people telling me about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I hope Ben has a little sister.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. I hope she can kick his ass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna get a new band-aid. Hey, how 'bout the time I cut the legs off your Malibu Ken?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They, uh, were infected. He wouldn't have made it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aw, my little nephew. Come here, little one. There's my little baby Ben. Hey, my little boy. Hey, he's not crying.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking fearfully at Joey) Hey, he's not crying.
Ben starts crying

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! There's still pie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm here. How's my little boy? Want Daddy to change your diaper? So, did you have fun with Uncle Joey and Uncle Chandler today?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, he rode the bus today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohhh. Big boy, riding the bus--Hey, I have a question. How come it says Property of Human Services on his butt?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You, you are gonna love this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Will you hold Ben for a sec? Come here. Come here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stay back, I've got kiwi. Run, Joey, Run!

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: (singing) Smelly cat, smelly cat, what are they feeding you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no. I'm sorry. It's "smelly cat, smel-ly cat".

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Smelly cat, smel-ly cat...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Better. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, much better. And you know what, don't feel bad, because it's a hard song.

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You wanna try it again?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Yeah. From the top?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, there is no top. That's the beauty of Smelly Cat. Um, why don't you just follow me?

lines.speaker: Stephanie
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mmmm hmmm.

lines.speaker: Together
lines.text: Smelly Cat, Smelly Cat, what are they feeding you? Smelly Cat, Smelly Cat, it's not your fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's too much. Sorry.
END
